macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Global stocks rally as AI optimism broadens beyond tech
items.summary: U.S. equities reached new records while European and Asian markets also advanced, with gains broadening across sectors as AI enthusiasm lifted sentiment. The move came alongside falling yields and narrower credit spreads, although oil ended the week lower in volatile trading.

items.title: Nvidia reports blowout Q1 revenue and announces major capital return plan
items.summary: Nvidia posted fiscal Q1 revenue of $81.6 billion, up 85% year over year and above expectations, reinforcing the strength of the AI-driven chip cycle. The company also announced a 25-fold dividend increase and an $80 billion buyback.

items.title: Markets await key U.S. inflation data, including the Fed’s preferred PCE gauge
items.summary: The upcoming U.S. PCE price index release is expected to show a 0.5% monthly increase and 3.8% annual inflation, with core PCE projected at 0.3% month over month and 3.3% year over year. Other closely watched releases include GDP, durable goods, jobless claims, and consumer confidence data.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Samsung unveils Galaxy Z TriFold with dual-hinge design and 10-inch display
items.summary: Samsung's newest foldable appears to push the category further with a redesigned dual-hinge mechanism, upgraded materials, and a larger screen that unfolds from a pocketable form factor. The device is being positioned as the company's most advanced foldable yet.

items.title: Amazon brings Alexa+ to the web
items.summary: Amazon is extending Alexa+ beyond its devices and onto the web, expanding the assistant’s reach as competition in consumer digital assistants intensifies. The move signals a broader push toward web-based and more agentic workflows.

items.title: Google and Qualcomm push Android PCs toward the mainstream
items.summary: Google and Qualcomm are working to scale Android from phones to desktops, aiming to challenge Microsoft and Apple in personal computing. The effort could reshape the PC market if software and hardware adoption gains momentum.

items.title: Quantum Motion deploys first full-stack quantum computer built with standard silicon fabrication
items.summary: Quantum Motion says it has deployed the first full-stack quantum computer built using standard silicon chip fabrication methods. If validated, the milestone could help make quantum systems more scalable and manufacturable.

items.title: Qualcomm launches Snapdragon W5+ Gen 2 and W5 Gen 2 for wearables
items.summary: Qualcomm's latest wearable platforms focus on improving battery life, design flexibility, and connectivity. The chips aim to remove some of the biggest barriers to wider wearable adoption.

items.title: Nvidia competition heats up as Spectral Compute touts new compiler technology
items.summary: A new compiler technology from Spectral Compute could broaden hardware options and reduce reliance on Nvidia's CUDA ecosystem. That could alter dynamics in the AI hardware market if developers adopt it widely.

items.title: AMD posts $9.2 billion quarter and presses advantage in enterprise computing
items.summary: AMD's latest quarter is being read as evidence that disciplined execution is helping the company gain ground on Intel and capitalize on gaps left by Nvidia's supply constraints. The results underscore AMD's growing role in enterprise and data center strategy.

items.title: Gartner warns organizations to block AI browsers over security risks
items.summary: Gartner is urging enterprises to restrict agentic browsing tools, warning they could expose sensitive data and weaken existing security controls. The advisory reflects growing concern about browser-based automation in corporate environments.
